metadata.name: Global Tariff Briefing
metadata.audience: NYT colleagues tracking tariffs and trade policy globally

all.headline: OPEC+ Agrees in Principle to Larger Oil Hike, Delegates Say
all.description: OPEC+ agreed in principle to a slightly larger increase in oil production next month, delegates said, as a conflict sparked by US-Israeli strikes on Iran threatened to bolster a rally in crude prices.

all.headline: Global Earnings Underscore Shift From US as S&P 500 Slumped
all.description: As the latest earnings season wraps, investors could be forgiven for missing some of the fireworks, what with AI’s stranglehold on the market narrative, geopolitical angst and renewed trade uncertaint

all.headline: Wall Street Turns to ‘Haven-First’ Strategy Amid Iran Crisis
all.description: The fast‑moving conflict across the Middle East is heightening investor anxiety and strengthening the case for safe‑haven trades such as Treasuries, gold and the Swiss franc.

all.headline: US Job Growth Seen Moderating After Robust January
all.description: US payrolls growth probably settled back in February after the strongest month of hiring in a year, returning to a more moderate and likely sustainable pace of hiring.

all.headline: Gulf Airlines Extend Flight Cancellations as Iran Targets Hubs
all.description: Airlines across the Persian Gulf extended their suspension of operations as retaliatory strikes from Iran enter a second day, causing major disruptions at some of the world’s busiest airports.

all.headline: Japan Shipping Companies Suspend Operations in Persian Gulf
all.description: Japanese shipping companies have suspended operations in the Persian Gulf as the military conflict in Iran escalates.

all.headline: How Starmer Stumbled Into a French-Style Fix for Britain’s Woes
all.description: The UK’s growth plan is simple. It wants more investment to deliver higher productivity that lifts wages and boosts living standards. There is an unfortunate consequence, however: unemployment.

all.headline: France’s CMA CGM Suspends Vessels’ Passage Through Suez Canal
all.description: France’s CMA CGM SA, the world’s third-largest container line, told vessels in the Persian Gulf to take shelter immediately and suspended passage through the Suez Canal.

all.headline: Iran Crisis Threatens Worst Disruption in Gas Markets Since 2022
all.description: A widening Middle East conflict looks set to create the most significant disruption for gas markets since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine upended global trade four years ago.

all.headline: Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei Dead at 86
all.description: Ali Khamenei, Iran’s anti-Western supreme leader who dominated the Islamic Republic for more than three decades, has died at 86. Bloomberg's Haslinda Amin explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

all.headline: Flights in and out of Middle East cancelled and diverted after Iran strikes
all.description: BA and Virgin Atlantic are among major airlines to ground services to the Middle East in light of the attacks.

all.headline: A firm hiring blind staff went bust - but its mission lives on
all.description: The business had been running for over 150 years before it collapsed, so one former employee took matters into her own hands.

all.headline: Could a huge data centre revitalise Ayrshire - or ruin it?
all.description: Controversial proposals to turn land near HMP Kilmarnock into a technology hub are being advanced by energy company ILI Group.

all.headline: The third man who links Andrew with Jeffrey Epstein
all.description: For nearly a decade, David Stern acted as a key conduit between Jeffrey Epstein and Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor.

all.headline: Trump orders government to stop using Anthropic in battle over AI use
all.description: The move announced on social media comes after a standoff between Anthropic's boss and the US Department of Defense.

all.headline: Why you can't get a signal at festivals and sports matches
all.description: Connecting up music and sports events to the internet is a massive undertaking.

all.headline: What the Warner Bros deal could mean for streaming, cinemas and news
all.description: If Paramount's takeover of Warner Bros goes ahead it could significantly reshape Hollywood.

all.headline: Brewdog expected to announce sale early next week
all.description: Staff are told Brewdog's German arm is not included in the sale and will now be liquidated but bars will trade as normal this weekend.

all.headline: Jack Dorsey's Block cuts thousands of jobs as it embraces AI
all.description: The Twitter co-founder says he believes the majority of firms will make similar changes "within the next year."

all.headline: Government to give go-ahead for £1bn defence helicopter deal
all.description: The chancellor has stepped in to protect jobs at the helicopter manufacturer in Somerset, the BBC understands.

all.headline: Ayatollah Khamenei's iron grip on power in Iran comes to an end
all.description: Iranian state TV confirms the supreme leader has been killed on the first day of massive US and Israeli air strikes on the country.

all.headline: What we know so far about the US-Israel attacks and Iran's retaliation
all.description: Iran responds by launching missiles and drones towards Israel and four Gulf Arab countries that host US military bases.

all.headline: Iran says US and Israel strikes hit school killing 108
all.description: There is no confirmation by the US or Israel of the strike on the school located near elite military troops.

all.headline: Luxury Dubai hotel hit as Iran launches retaliatory strikes across region
all.description: Smoke and flames rose from Dubai's Fairmont The Palm hotel as civilian targets came under fire along with the US military bases across the Middle East.

all.headline: Where have Nepal's 'nepo kids' gone as corruption takes centre stage in election?
all.description: The excess of politicians' children was front and centre of the anger that drove protests that toppled the government last year.

all.headline: Wellness peptide craze: Why people are injecting drugs 'not for human consumption'
all.description: Growing numbers of people are injecting unregulated peptides for health reasons - but one expert says they are "lab rats".

all.headline: Why is WhatsApp's privacy policy facing a legal challenge in India?
all.description: A 2021 policy update requires users to share data with Meta for ads purposes to continue using the app.

all.headline: At least 20 killed after military plane carrying banknotes crashes in Bolivia
all.description: Police fire tear gas to disperse crowds allegedly trying to take banknotes from the crash site.

all.headline: Bill Clinton asked about hot tub photo and testifies he knew 'nothing' of Epstein crimes
all.description: The former president told the committee that he would never have flown on Epstein's plane if he "had any inkling of what he was doing".

all.headline: More than 40,000 Hong Kong cabbies register to accept Octopus payment

Why Chinese honour Toilet Goddess with straw effigies, rituals during Lantern Festival
Pakistani, Afghan clashes widen as Arab states struggle to halt war

China poised to earn vast profits from global energy transition: analysts

Chairman of China’s second-largest liquor producer under corruption probe
Belgium seizes suspected Russian shadow fleet vessel
all.description: Europe is ramping up the targeting of Moscow's fleet of vessels suspected of carrying sanctioned oil or damaging undersea infrastructure.

all.headline: Iran strikes threaten to deplete US weapons supplies — and put American troops at risk
all.description: The Trump administration already burned through significant stockpiles of air defense missiles in operations against Iran and its proxy groups.

all.headline: Ayatollah Khamenei is dead. Here’s what that means for Iran’s leadership.
all.description: The country’s supreme leader led the Islamic Republic for 37 years.

all.headline: Iranian supreme leader killed in airstrike, leaving power vacuum atop regime
all.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's death throws the future of the regime into doubt.

all.headline: Putin’s friendship has limits — as Iran just found out
all.description: Leaders in Damascus, Caracas and now Tehran have all discovered that Russian support only goes so far.

all.headline: Trump’s patience runs out: Inside the final days before the strike on Iran
all.description: President Donald Trump, already upset with Tehran’s violent crackdown, gave diplomacy a final shot, but ultimately decided time was up.

all.headline: US Gulf allies rattled — and seeing few good options — following Iran strikes
all.description: Iran’s retaliation across the region forces Washington’s Arab partners — long opposed to strikes — to weigh deeper involvement as U.S. assets come under fire.

all.headline: EU scrambles for unity as Iran crisis exposes transatlantic rift
all.description: Brussels has shifted into crisis mode, with emergency meetings piling up Saturday and Sunday.

all.headline: Satellite imagery of devastation at Iranian supreme leader’s residence
all.description: Photos appear to show smoke rising from the office building of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

all.headline: Poland says it had advance knowledge of US-Israel strikes on Iran
all.description: Unlike Paris, Warsaw and Berlin were told of the strikes before they happened, exposing differences in how Europe was looped in.

Ambassador Greer Spoke to University of Virginia Law Students on How International Law Can Best Serve Peace and Prosperity

primary.headline: OPEC+ Agrees in Principle to Larger Oil Hike, Delegates Say
primary.description: OPEC+ agreed in principle to a slightly larger increase in oil production next month, delegates said, as a conflict sparked by US-Israeli strikes on Iran threatened to bolster a rally in crude prices.

primary.headline: Global Earnings Underscore Shift From US as S&P 500 Slumped
primary.description: As the latest earnings season wraps, investors could be forgiven for missing some of the fireworks, what with AI’s stranglehold on the market narrative, geopolitical angst and renewed trade uncertaint

primary.headline: Wall Street Turns to ‘Haven-First’ Strategy Amid Iran Crisis
primary.description: The fast‑moving conflict across the Middle East is heightening investor anxiety and strengthening the case for safe‑haven trades such as Treasuries, gold and the Swiss franc.

primary.headline: US Job Growth Seen Moderating After Robust January
primary.description: US payrolls growth probably settled back in February after the strongest month of hiring in a year, returning to a more moderate and likely sustainable pace of hiring.

primary.headline: Gulf Airlines Extend Flight Cancellations as Iran Targets Hubs
primary.description: Airlines across the Persian Gulf extended their suspension of operations as retaliatory strikes from Iran enter a second day, causing major disruptions at some of the world’s busiest airports.

primary.headline: Japan Shipping Companies Suspend Operations in Persian Gulf
primary.description: Japanese shipping companies have suspended operations in the Persian Gulf as the military conflict in Iran escalates.

primary.headline: How Starmer Stumbled Into a French-Style Fix for Britain’s Woes
primary.description: The UK’s growth plan is simple. It wants more investment to deliver higher productivity that lifts wages and boosts living standards. There is an unfortunate consequence, however: unemployment.

primary.headline: France’s CMA CGM Suspends Vessels’ Passage Through Suez Canal
primary.description: France’s CMA CGM SA, the world’s third-largest container line, told vessels in the Persian Gulf to take shelter immediately and suspended passage through the Suez Canal.

primary.headline: Iran Crisis Threatens Worst Disruption in Gas Markets Since 2022
primary.description: A widening Middle East conflict looks set to create the most significant disruption for gas markets since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine upended global trade four years ago.

primary.headline: Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei Dead at 86
primary.description: Ali Khamenei, Iran’s anti-Western supreme leader who dominated the Islamic Republic for more than three decades, has died at 86. Bloomberg's Haslinda Amin explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

secondary.headline: Flights in and out of Middle East cancelled and diverted after Iran strikes
secondary.description: BA and Virgin Atlantic are among major airlines to ground services to the Middle East in light of the attacks.

secondary.headline: A firm hiring blind staff went bust - but its mission lives on
secondary.description: The business had been running for over 150 years before it collapsed, so one former employee took matters into her own hands.

secondary.headline: Could a huge data centre revitalise Ayrshire - or ruin it?
secondary.description: Controversial proposals to turn land near HMP Kilmarnock into a technology hub are being advanced by energy company ILI Group.

secondary.headline: The third man who links Andrew with Jeffrey Epstein
secondary.description: For nearly a decade, David Stern acted as a key conduit between Jeffrey Epstein and Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor.

secondary.description: The move announced on social media comes after a standoff between Anthropic's boss and the US Department of Defense.

secondary.headline: Why you can't get a signal at festivals and sports matches
secondary.description: Connecting up music and sports events to the internet is a massive undertaking.

secondary.headline: What the Warner Bros deal could mean for streaming, cinemas and news
secondary.description: If Paramount's takeover of Warner Bros goes ahead it could significantly reshape Hollywood.

secondary.headline: Brewdog expected to announce sale early next week
secondary.description: Staff are told Brewdog's German arm is not included in the sale and will now be liquidated but bars will trade as normal this weekend.

secondary.headline: Jack Dorsey's Block cuts thousands of jobs as it embraces AI
secondary.description: The Twitter co-founder says he believes the majority of firms will make similar changes "within the next year."

secondary.description: The chancellor has stepped in to protect jobs at the helicopter manufacturer in Somerset, the BBC understands.

secondary.headline: Ayatollah Khamenei's iron grip on power in Iran comes to an end
secondary.description: Iranian state TV confirms the supreme leader has been killed on the first day of massive US and Israeli air strikes on the country.

secondary.headline: What we know so far about the US-Israel attacks and Iran's retaliation
secondary.description: Iran responds by launching missiles and drones towards Israel and four Gulf Arab countries that host US military bases.

secondary.headline: Iran says US and Israel strikes hit school killing 108
secondary.description: There is no confirmation by the US or Israel of the strike on the school located near elite military troops.

secondary.headline: Luxury Dubai hotel hit as Iran launches retaliatory strikes across region
secondary.description: Smoke and flames rose from Dubai's Fairmont The Palm hotel as civilian targets came under fire along with the US military bases across the Middle East.

secondary.headline: Where have Nepal's 'nepo kids' gone as corruption takes centre stage in election?
secondary.description: The excess of politicians' children was front and centre of the anger that drove protests that toppled the government last year.

secondary.headline: Wellness peptide craze: Why people are injecting drugs 'not for human consumption'
secondary.description: Growing numbers of people are injecting unregulated peptides for health reasons - but one expert says they are "lab rats".

secondary.headline: Why is WhatsApp's privacy policy facing a legal challenge in India?
secondary.description: A 2021 policy update requires users to share data with Meta for ads purposes to continue using the app.

secondary.headline: At least 20 killed after military plane carrying banknotes crashes in Bolivia
secondary.description: Police fire tear gas to disperse crowds allegedly trying to take banknotes from the crash site.

secondary.headline: Bill Clinton asked about hot tub photo and testifies he knew 'nothing' of Epstein crimes
secondary.description: The former president told the committee that he would never have flown on Epstein's plane if he "had any inkling of what he was doing".

secondary.headline: Iran strikes threaten to deplete US weapons supplies — and put American troops at risk

secondary.headline: Poland says it had advance knowledge of US-Israel strikes on Iran
